Available agent types for the Agent tool:
- claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)
- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)
- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)
- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:

- lark-approval: 飞书审批：当前用户审批的查询与全部处理操作，覆盖待本人审批的任务与本人发起的实例。审批待办不是飞书任务（任务类待办走 lark-task）；不负责创建审批定义和发起新审批。
- lark-apps: 妙搭（Spark/Miaoda）应用开发与托管：应用创建、HTML静态站点发布、本地全栈开发、云端生成迭代。当用户要开发/新建一个系统·工具·平台·应用，或要本地开发 / 云端开发 / 修改 / 部署 / 发布 / 上线 / 拿可分享链接，或用 HTML 做页面·网站给人看，或提到妙搭/Spark/Miaoda、应用数据库、可见范围时使用。不负责普通云盘文件上传（lark-drive）、飞书文档编辑（lark-doc）、原生幻灯片创建（lark-slides）。
- lark-attendance: 飞书考勤打卡：查询自己的考勤打卡记录
- lark-base: 飞书多维表格（Base）操作：建表、字段、记录、视图、统计、公式/lookup、表单、仪表盘、workflow、角色权限；遇到 Base/多维表格/bitable 或 /base/ 链接时使用。文件导入转 lark-drive，认证/授权转 lark-shared。
- lark-calendar: 飞书日历：管理日历日程和会议室。查看/搜索日程、创建/更新日程、管理参会人、查询忙闲和推荐时段、预定会议室。当用户需要查看日程安排、创建/修改会议、查询/预定会议室时使用。不负责：查询过去的视频会议记录（走 lark-vc）、待办任务（走 lark-task）。
- lark-contact: 飞书 / Lark 通讯录:按姓名 / 邮箱解析成 open_id,或按 open_id 反查姓名 / 部门 / 邮箱 / 联系方式 / 个人状态 / 签名。当用户提到某人姓名要下一步发消息 / 排日程,或拿到 open_id 想查具体信息时使用。不负责部门树遍历、按部门列员工、组织架构图,这类需求走原生 OpenAPI。
- lark-doc: 飞书云文档（Docx / Wiki 文档，v2 API）：读取和编辑飞书文档内容。当用户给出文档 URL 或 token，或需要查看、创建、编辑文档、插入或下载文档图片附件时使用。文档中嵌入的电子表格、多维表格、画板，先用本 skill 提取 token 再切到对应 skill。当用户给出 doubao.com 的 /docx/ 或 /wiki/ URL/token 时，也应直接使用本 skill；路由依据是 URL 路径模式和 token，而不是域名。不负责文档评论管理，也不负责表格或 Base 的数据操作。
- lark-drive: 飞书云空间（云盘/云存储）：管理 Drive 文件和文件夹，包含上传/下载、创建文件夹、复制/移动/删除、查看元数据、评论/权限/订阅、标题、版本和本地文件导入。用户需要整理云盘目录、处理云空间资源 URL/token，或导入 Word/Markdown/Excel/CSV/PPTX/.base 为 docx/sheet/bitable/slides 时使用；doubao.com 云空间 URL/token 也按资源路径和 token 路由，不回退 WebFetch。不负责：文档内容编辑（走 lark-doc）、表格/Base 表内数据操作（走 lark-sheets/lark-base）、知识空间节点/成员管理（走 lark-wiki）、原生 Markdown 文件读写/patch/diff（走 lark-markdown）。
- lark-event: Lark/Feishu real-time event listening / subscribing / consuming: stream events as NDJSON via `lark-cli event consume <EventKey>` (covers IM messages/reactions/chat changes, VC meeting ended, Minutes generated, Whiteboard updated, etc.). Use for Lark bots, real-time message processing, long-running subscribers, streaming webhook/push handlers. Supports `--max-events` / `--timeout` bounded runs and a stderr ready-marker contract — designed for AI agents running as subprocesses.
- lark-im: 飞书即时通讯：收发消息和管理群聊。发送和回复消息、搜索聊天记录、管理群聊成员、上传下载图片和文件（支持大文件分片下载）、管理表情回复、发送应用内/短信/电话加急。当用户需要发消息、查看或搜索聊天记录、下载聊天中的文件、查看群成员、搜索群、创建群聊或话题群、管理标记数据、管理 Feed 置顶（添加/移除/查询置顶会话）、管理标签数据时使用。
- lark-mail: 飞书邮箱 — draft, compose, send, reply, forward, read, and search emails; manage drafts, folders, labels, contacts, attachments, and mail rules. Use when user mentions 起草邮件, 写一封邮件, 拟邮件, 草稿, 发通知邮件, 发送邮件, 发邮件, 回复邮件, 转发邮件, 查看邮件, 看邮件, 读邮件, 搜索邮件, 查邮件, 收件箱, 邮件会话, 编辑草稿, 管理草稿, 下载附件, 邮件文件夹, 邮件标签, 邮件联系人, 监听新邮件, 收信规则, 邮件规则, draft, compose, send email, reply, forward, inbox, mail thread, mail rules.
- lark-markdown: 飞书 Markdown：查看、创建、上传、编辑和比较 Markdown 文件。当用户需要创建或编辑 Markdown 文件、读取、修改、局部 patch 或比较差异时使用。不负责将 Markdown 导入为飞书在线文档，也不负责文件搜索、权限、评论、移动、删除等云空间管理操作。
- lark-minutes: 飞书妙记：搜索妙记列表、查看妙记基础信息、下载妙记音视频文件、上传音视频生成妙记、更新妙记标题、替换说话人。当需要获取、操作或者生成妙记时使用。也支持将本地音视频文件转成纪要和逐字稿（优先使用本 skill，不要用 ffmpeg/whisper 本地转写）。不负责：获取会议关联妙记，或仅按自然语言标题定位纪要
- lark-okr: 飞书 OKR：管理目标与关键结果。查看和编辑 OKR 周期、目标、关键结果、对齐关系、量化指标和进展记录。当用户需要查看或创建 OKR、管理目标和关键结果、查看对齐关系时使用。不负责：待办任务管理（lark-task）、日程/会议安排（lark-calendar）、绩效评估
- lark-openapi-explorer: 飞书/Lark 原生 OpenAPI 探索：从官方文档库中挖掘未经 CLI 封装的原生 OpenAPI 接口。当用户的需求无法被现有 lark-* skill 或 lark-cli 已注册命令满足，需要查找并调用原生飞书 OpenAPI 时使用。
- lark-shared: Use when first setting up lark-cli, running auth login, switching user/bot identity (--as), handling permission denied or scope errors, needing to update lark-cli, or seeing _notice in JSON output.
- lark-sheets: 飞书电子表格：创建和操作电子表格。支持创建表格、管理工作表与行列结构（增删/合并/调整尺寸/隐藏/冻结）、读写单元格（值/公式/样式/批注/单元格图片）、查找替换、多操作原子批量更新，以及图表、透视表、条件格式、筛选器、迷你图、浮动图片等对象的创建与维护。当用户需要创建电子表格、管理工作表、批量读写或编辑数据、统计汇总与可视化、表格美化、公式计算（含 Excel 公式迁移）等任务时使用。若用户是想按名称或关键词搜索云空间（云盘/云存储）里的表格文件，请改用 lark-drive 的 drive +search 先定位资源。当用户给出 doubao.com 的 /sheets/ URL/token 时，也应直接使用本 skill，不要因为域名不是飞书而回退到 WebFetch；路由依据是 URL 路径模式和 token，而不是域名。仅针对飞书在线电子表格，不适用于本地 Excel 文件。
- lark-skill-maker: 创建 lark-cli 的自定义 Skill。当用户需要把飞书 API 操作封装成可复用的 Skill（包装原子 API 或编排多步流程）时使用。
- lark-slides: 飞书幻灯片：创建和编辑幻灯片。创建演示文稿、读取幻灯片内容、管理幻灯片页面（创建、删除、读取、局部替换）。当用户需要创建或编辑幻灯片、读取或修改单个页面时使用。当用户给出 doubao.com 的 /slides/ URL/token 时，也应直接使用本 skill，不要因为域名不是飞书而回退到 WebFetch；路由依据是 URL 路径模式和 token，而不是域名。不负责：云文档内容编辑（走 lark-doc）、云文档里的独立画板对象（走 lark-whiteboard，注意 slide 内嵌的流程图/架构图仍属本 skill）、上传或下载普通文件（走 lark-drive）。
- lark-task: 飞书任务：管理任务、清单和任务智能体。创建待办任务、查看和更新任务状态、拆分子任务、组织任务清单、分配协作成员、上传任务附件、注册或注销任务智能体、更新任务智能体的主页数据、写入智能体任务记录。当用户需要创建待办事项、查看任务列表、跟踪任务进度、管理项目清单或给他人分配任务、为任务上传附件文件、注册注销任务智能体、更新智能体主页数据、写入任务记录时使用。
- lark-vc: 飞书视频会议：搜索历史会议记录、查询会议纪要（总结/待办/章节/逐字稿）、查询参会人快照。当用户查询已结束的会议、获取会议产物（纪要/妙记）、查看参会人时使用；查询未来日程走 lark-calendar。不负责：Agent 真实入会/离会、会中实时事件（走 lark-vc-agent）。
- lark-vc-agent: 飞书视频会议会中能力：用于让应用机器人真实加入或离开正在进行的会议，并读取当前身份可见的会中事件，如参会人加入/离开、发言、聊天、屏幕共享。适用于用户询问正在开的会议发生了什么、谁在发言、是否共享内容，或需要发现当前可读的进行中会议 ID。不负责已结束会议搜索、参会人快照、纪要、逐字稿或录制查询，这些使用 lark-vc 技能。
- lark-whiteboard: 飞书画板：查询和编辑飞书云文档中的画板。支持导出画板为预览图片、导出原始节点结构、使用多种格式更新画板内容。 当用户需要查看画板内容、导出画板图片、编辑画板时使用此 skill。不负责：飞书云文档内容编辑（lark-doc）、文档内嵌电子表格/Base（lark-sheets / lark-base）。
- lark-wiki: 飞书知识库：管理知识空间、空间成员和文档节点。创建和查询知识空间、查看和管理空间成员、管理节点层级结构、在知识库中组织文档和快捷方式。当用户需要在知识库中查找或创建文档、浏览知识空间结构、查看或管理空间成员、移动或复制节点时使用。当用户给出 doubao.com 的 /wiki/ URL/token 时，也应直接使用本 skill，不要因为域名不是飞书而回退到 WebFetch；路由依据是 URL 路径模式和 token，而不是域名。不负责：上传文件到知识库节点下（走 lark-drive）、编辑文档/表格/Base 内容（走 lark-doc / lark-sheets / lark-base）。
- lark-workflow-meeting-summary: 会议纪要整理工作流：汇总指定时间范围内的会议纪要并生成结构化报告。当用户需要整理会议纪要、生成会议周报、回顾一段时间内的会议内容时使用。
- lark-workflow-standup-report: 日程待办摘要：编排 calendar +agenda 和 task +get-my-tasks，生成指定日期的日程与未完成任务摘要。适用于了解今天/明天/本周的安排。
- frontend-design: tracelab 前端视觉设计与 i18n 规范（Tailwind + shadcn/ui，暗色 DevTools 风，中英双语）。写任何样式/文案前先读。
- frontend-mvvm: tracelab 前端 MVVM 架构与代码规范（Vite+React+TS / Zustand / TanStack Query / React Router）。写任何前端代码前先读。
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# tracelab — work log & notes
2	
3	A running summary of what's built, the key decisions, and the problems found +
4	fixed. Newest phase on top. (Architecture lives in `CONVENTIONS.md` and the
5	`.claude/skills/`; this file is the narrative + gotchas.)
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Phase: codex hooks → flow graph → routing → diff → replay → launch → replay lib
10	
11	### What's delivered
12	
13	**Capture**
14	- HTTP reverse-proxy capture (cc + codex) and **harness hooks** for both runtimes.
15	  - cc hooks via `--settings`; **codex hooks** via `-c hooks.<E>=[...]` (TOML) +
16	    `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` (per-invocation, no `~/.codex/config.toml`
17	    write). codex events carry `tool_use_id` == responses `call_id`.
18	  - `tool_name` extracted from hook payloads → shown on flow cards.
19	  - Hooks are **receipt-timestamped** (RFC3339Nano, same as HTTP) so they
20	    interleave on the timeline.
21	
22	**Viewer (React, MVVM)**
23	- **Flow graph (hook-first)**: a turn's spine is the hook timeline; each hook
24	  card shows its payload inline (folded by default, key fields surfaced). HTTP is
25	  not a first-layer node — a `request #N` chip opens the exchange in a **side
26	  sheet**. Correlation is structural (no jumping): hook→request by strict causal
27	  ordering (tool hook → producing completion; UserPromptSubmit → triggered
28	  request), `is_completion` only.
29	- **Context diff in flow**: a ⇄ on the request chip opens the Diff tab; semantic
30	  diff shows a one-line change classification (+N tool results / system changed /
31	  suspected compaction).
32	- **URL routing**: navigation state lives in the URL (`/sessions/:id?tab=…&
33	  req_id=…&turn_id=…`), shareable + reload-safe; Go viewer has a real SPA
34	  fallback for deep links.
35	- **Replay**: re-send a captured completion (optionally edited body, CodeMirror
36	  JSON editor) to upstream, normalize, compare original vs replay. Two-step
37	  confirm; not persisted.
38	
39	**Launch page (`/launch`)** — opt-in
40	- Start cc/codex through the proxy in a chosen terminal app (auto-detected:
41	  Terminal/iTerm/Ghostty/…). Subscription or API-key mode.
42	- Always shows a copy-paste "run it yourself" command.
43	
44	**Replay library (`/cases`)** — eval groundwork
45	- `replay_cases` table + predefined "你是谁" seeds (one per wire format) + "save
46	  as case" from captures. Run a case against a chosen **live session** (supplies
47	  credentials), editable body, reuses the replay pipeline. Not persisted.
48	
49	### Key design decisions
50	- **Credentials in process memory only, never on disk.** Captured auth headers
51	  and API-key-mode inject auth live in `Sessions` (memory); they die with the
52	  process. Consequence: only sessions captured/registered in the *current* serve
53	  process are replayable (others → 409).
54	- **API-key launch = proxy-inject.** The agent gets a placeholder key; the proxy
55	  swaps in the real key (held in memory) on forward — key never reaches the
56	  agent/terminal/disk. The copy-paste manual command keeps the key in the user's
57	  own shell instead.
58	- **Replay reuses capture's normalize pipeline** — resend verbatim bytes, build a
59	  SourceEvent, run the same registry. No provider-specific replay code.
60	- **Correlation is exact, not heuristic** — based on the fixed tool-call
61	  lifecycle ordering, filtered to real completions.
62	- **Replay library is "grown", not separate** — a case is provider-neutral
63	  request material; a session supplies credentials at run time.
64	
65	### Security model (Launch)
66	- Server-launch is **opt-in**: `-allow-launch`, OFF by default. Without it the
67	  server executes nothing; the page only shows the command to run yourself.
68	- **Cross-origin POSTs rejected** (Origin check) — blocks CSRF / DNS-rebinding
69	  from triggering local exec.
70	- Working dir is validated before launch (clear 400, no silent `cd` failure).
71	
72	### Problems found & fixed
73	- Hook events had **empty `started_at`** → all hooks sorted before all HTTP and
74	  correlation computed null. Fix: stamp receipt time + idempotent migration
75	  backfilling old rows from `created_at`.
76	- Correlation initially included **control/probe requests** (`is_completion=
77	  false`) → hooks linked to a probe. Fix: only real completions are targets.
78	- A stray `./tracelab serve` (no `-data`) squatted :8787 with an empty dir →

1	# tracelab
2	
3	Local-first lab for studying coding-agent ↔ LLM traffic (Claude Code, Codex, …).
4	A clean rebuild of the `aethertrace` experiment, addressing the issues in
5	`../aethertrace/next.md`.
6	
7	> **Status: Module 1 — capture + normalize.** This delivers the foundation only:
8	> collect exchanges from multiple sources and normalize them into one
9	> provider-neutral model. Storage/tagging (M2), the React viewer (M3), and the
10	> material/eval library (M4) build on top later, module by module (per next.md 9.1).
11	
12	## The one idea: decouple "how data arrives" from "what it means"
13	
14	```
15	 source adapters            event.SourceEvent          normalize layer
16	 ──────────────             ─────────────────          ───────────────
17	 httpcap (reverse proxy) ┐                         ┌─ anthropic
18	 hook (harness events)   ├──►  raw, provider-  ──► ├─ openai-responses (Codex)
19	 (importers, later)      ┘     agnostic facts      └─ openai-chat
20	                                     │                      │
21	                                     ▼                      ▼
22	                                   sink  ◄────  NormalizedEnvelope
23	                              (JSONL now, DB later)   (typed blocks, usage,
24	                                                       section %, tag signals)
25	```
26	
27	- **`internal/event`** is the stable boundary. It carries raw HTTP/hook facts and
28	  **no provider semantics**. Adding a source or a provider never touches the other.
29	- Adapters and providers are independent implementations behind small interfaces;
30	  they share only atomic helpers (`normalize/shared`). See `CONVENTIONS.md`.
31	
32	## What Module 1 already does (mapped to next.md)
33	
34	| next.md | delivered |
35	| --- | --- |
36	| 1.1 don't mutate global config | launcher uses env (cc) / `-c` single-run overrides (codex); never writes `~/.codex/config.toml` |
37	| 1.2 / 7.1 decouple sources; hooks | `httpcap` + `hook` adapters emit the same `SourceEvent` |
38	| 3.1 concurrent cc/codex sessions | per-session token → `correlation.session_id`; one proxy, isolated sessions |
39	| 3.3 / 4.1 no keyword/hard-coded parsing | content blocks typed from provider structure; uncertain tags marked `suspected` (疑似) |
40	| 4.x token usage / section ratio | `usage` + per-section approximate token share (system/tools/messages) |
41	| 8.2 Go layering | layered packages, independent providers, file-size limits |
42	
43	## Run it
44	
45	Build:
46	
47	```bash
48	go build -o tracelab ./cmd/tracelab
49	```
50	
51	Start the capture service:
52	
53	```bash
54	./tracelab serve -listen 127.0.0.1:8787 -out traces.jsonl
55	```
56	
57	Launch a client through it (nothing global is modified):
58	
59	```bash
60	./tracelab launch -kind cc    -- <claude args>
61	./tracelab launch -kind codex -- <codex args>
62	```
63	
64	Inspect captured + normalized records (provider, model, section token %, signals):
65	
66	```bash
67	./tracelab dump traces.jsonl
68	```
69	
70	Harness hooks can post to the same pipeline:
71	
72	```bash
73	curl -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:8787/_hook?runtime=claude_code&event=PreToolUse&session=<id>' \
74	  --data-binary @hook.json
75	```
76	
77	## Test
78	
79	```bash
80	go test ./...
81	```
82	
83	Golden tests run the real captured fixtures in `testdata/` through the
84	normalizers; `internal/server` exercises the full capture→normalize→sink path
85	with concurrent cc + codex sessions; `internal/source/httpcap` asserts the
86	launcher is non-invasive.
87	
88	## Not in Module 1
89	
90	DB + tag persistence (M2); React viewer with terminal-style session tabs,
91	search, jq-style explore, token-ratio charts, replay (M3); material + eval
92	library (M4). Design notes for those live in `../aethertrace/next.md` and the
93	approved plan.

content.text:
看完 README / WORKLOG / 约定文档，我对这个项目有比较具体的判断了。直接说，不灌鸡汤：

## 有意义，而且切口选得不错

核心价值在一句话：**它盯的是"coding agent ↔ LLM 之间的流量"这个别人没怎么认真做的缝。**

市面上 LLM 可观测性工具一大堆（Langfuse / Helicone / LangSmith / Braintrust…），但它们几乎都假设"你在写自己的 LLM 应用、自己埋点"。而 tracelab 是反过来的：**把已经存在的、闭源的 coding agent（Claude Code、Codex）当黑盒，从外面截流量 + 收 harness hook，再跨 provider 归一化。** 这个角度是真稀缺的，做得好的人很少。

具体几个点我认为是真有价值、不是自嗨：

1. **"每一轮到底往上下文里塞了什么"** —— flow graph + context diff + 分 section 的 token 占比。任何调 agent 的人都想看这个，但平时根本看不到。compaction 检测、context 膨胀可视化是刚需。
2. **hook↔HTTP 的结构化因果关联（非启发式）** —— 基于 tool-call 生命周期严格定序，而不是猜。这是工程上有含金量的判断，做对了不容易。
3. **降智检测 / 同 case 跨时间跨模型回归（M4 方向）** —— "模型是不是偷偷变笨了"是一个被广泛感知、却几乎没有顺手工具去证伪/证实的问题。谁能把它做成一键对比矩阵，谁就有话题度。
4. **provider-neutral 的归一化边界**（`internal/event`）—— 加 source 和加 provider 互不影响。这个架构纪律说明你不是在堆 demo。

## 会侵蚀它"意义"的几个风险，也得诚实讲

- **官方自己会补这块。** Claude Code / Codex 迟早出更深的 trace。你的护城河只能是：**跨 runtime、本地、可 hack、给研究者用**——而不是"给普通用户看 token"。要守住这个定位。
- **replay/eval 打的是真实计费接口、凭证只在内存** —— 思路干净，但天然限制了规模化和"放给别人用"。它更像是一个**强力的个人研究台**，不是一个能长成产品的东西。这没问题，但你要清楚它是哪一种。
- **它本身写着是 `aethertrace` 实验的"clean rebuild"** —— 也就是说它有相当一部分意义是"我借这个把架构/归一化/agent 内部机制吃透"。**作为学习和研究载体，它的意义几乎不依赖于会不会火。**

## 我的结论

- 如果你的目标是**搞懂 coding agent 内部怎么跟模型交互、顺便练架构**：非常值得继续，已经超过"玩具"门槛了。
- 如果你想让它**对别人有用 / 有传播**：把宝押在 **M4 的"降智检测 + 同 case 跨模型对比矩阵"**上——这是唯一一个普通开发者会主动搜、会转发的钩子。capture/viewer 是地基，eval 才是能讲出去的故事。
- 唯一要避免的失败模式：把它做成"又一个 token 计量面板"。那个赛道已经满了。

要不要我帮你把 M4（eval/对比矩阵/断言评分）拆成一个具体的落地计划？那部分目前在 WORKLOG 里还只是 "Pending / next" 一行字，是这个项目从"个人研究台"走向"有人用"的关键一跳。

# claude-tap: Local Proxy & Trace Viewer for AI Coding Agents

The **claude-tap** project (by liaohch3 on GitHub) is a tool designed to intercept and inspect the API traffic generated by AI coding agents. Here's an overview based on the search results:

## What It Does

claude-tap is a local proxy and trace viewer for AI coding agents. You run your CLI through it, or listen to local app transcripts, then inspect the real API traffic and agent context: system prompts, conversation history, tool schemas, tool calls, and more.

It's quite broad in its client support. It works with Claude Code, Codex CLI, Codex App, Gemini CLI, Kimi CLI, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Pi, Hermes Agent, Cursor CLI, Qoder CLI, Antigravity CLI, and CodeBuddy CLI. You can open a real agent run, inspect every request, and compare how context changes between turns.

## How It Works (Architecture)

Based on a related fork, the core mechanism works as follows: claude-tap starts a reverse proxy and spawns the selected client (claude or codex) with the provider-specific base URL pointing to it. All API requests flow through the proxy → upstream API → back through proxy, and SSE streaming responses are forwarded in real-time.

Each interaction is logged: Each request-response pair is recorded to trace.jsonl. On exit, a self-contained HTML viewer is generated, and live mode (optional) broadcasts updates to the browser via SSE.

Key features highlighted include: API keys auto-redacted, zero latency, a self-contained viewer, and real-time live mode.

## Proxy Modes

claude-tap supports two proxy modes — **reverse** (default) and **forward**. The forward mode is important for certain scenarios:

- **Gemini CLI**: Gemini CLI uses forward proxy mode by default, because Google OAuth / Code Assist traffic goes to several Google endpoints, so forward proxy capture is the safest default.

- **OpenCode**: Because it can talk to many providers, claude-tap defaults to forward proxy mode for opencode: it injects HTTPS_PROXY plus the local CA into the child process so traffic to any provider is captured.

- **AWS Bedrock**: When the endpoint is a real AWS domain (*.amazonaws.com), claude-tap does not rewrite ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL to localhost — doing so would break AWS SigV4 signature validation. Forward proxy mode is used to capture this traffic without modifying the signed request.

## Installation & Usage

It requires Python 3.11+ and the client you want to trace. The recommended install is via uv (`uv tool install claude-tap`) or pip (`pip install claude-tap`), and you then run the client you want to inspect through claude-tap.

For Claude Code specifically: you use claude-tap in place of claude, such as `claude-tap -- <claude-args>`; no separate --tap-client value is needed. For the Claude Code VS Code extension, you set the Claude Process Wrapper to claude-tap.

## Additional Capabilities

It also supports custom/local upstreams: if a tool such as CC Switch points Claude Code at a local ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, claude-tap detects that value from Claude settings and records the traffic before forwarding it upstream.

The project also feeds into related tooling: Phistory archives versioned system prompt snapshots from agent CLIs such as Claude Code, Codex, Kimi, opencode, and Pi, using claude-tap's capture-only prompt export to preserve raw HTTP trace evidence and generate comparison-friendly prompt snapshots.

---

**Note:** There are several distinct GitHub projects with similar names. Be aware that **claude-tap** (liaohch3) is focused on *inspecting/tracing* traffic, whereas projects literally named **claude-code-proxy** (e.g., by 1rgs, raine, horselock) are generally aimed at *routing* Claude Code to alternative model backends like OpenAI, Gemini, or DeepSeek rather than traffic inspection. The main repository for claude-tap is at `github.com/liaohch3/claude-tap`.

# claude-tap

**claude-tap** is an open-source tool by developer **liaohch3** for intercepting and inspecting the API traffic of AI coding agents. Here's what I found.

## What it does

It's a CLI tool that wraps Claude Code with a local reverse proxy to intercept and record all API requests. It's useful for studying Claude Code's Context Engineering — how it constructs system prompts, manages conversation history, selects tools, and optimizes token usage across turns.

While originally focused on Claude Code, it has expanded considerably. It works with Claude Code, Codex CLI, Codex App, Gemini CLI, Kimi CLI, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Pi, Hermes Agent, Cursor CLI, Qoder CLI, Antigravity CLI, and CodeBuddy CLI.

## How it works

The architecture is straightforward. claude-tap starts a reverse proxy and spawns the selected client (claude or codex) with the provider-specific base URL pointing to it. All API requests flow through the proxy → upstream API → back through proxy. SSE streaming responses are forwarded in real-time (zero added latency). Each request-response pair is recorded to trace.jsonl, and on exit, a self-contained HTML viewer is generated.

More specifically for Claude Code, it starts a local HTTP reverse proxy (127.0.0.1:PORT), launches Claude Code with ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL pointing to it, forwards requests to api.anthropic.com via HTTPS, and records each request-response pair to a JSONL trace file.

## Key features

- **Debug behavior with evidence:** compare adjacent requests and pinpoint which prompt, message, tool, or parameter changed.
- **Share one portable artifact:** each run writes a local trace session that can be exported to a self-contained HTML viewer for review or archiving.
- **Keep traces on your machine:** no hosted dashboard is required, and common auth headers are redacted before recording.

The viewer itself offers useful inspection capabilities, including model grouping (sidebar groups requests by Opus > Sonnet > Haiku), a token usage breakdown (input/output/cache read/cache creation), a tool inspector with expandable cards, full-text search across messages, tools, prompts, and responses, dark mode, and i18n support.

## Installation

It requires Python 3.11+ and the client you want to trace. Install with `uv tool install claude-tap` (recommended) or `pip install claude-tap`.

## Basic usage

For Claude Code: use claude-tap in place of claude, such as `claude-tap -- <claude-args>`; no separate --tap-client value is needed. Some examples:

`claude-tap` (basic launch), `claude-tap --tap-live` (watch API calls in real-time in browser), `claude-tap -- --model claude-opus-4-6` (pass flags through), and `claude-tap -- --dangerously-skip-permissions` (auto-accept tool calls).

It also handles custom upstreams: claude-tap auto-detects custom Claude Code upstreams from ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL or ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL in your environment or Claude settings. Use --tap-target only when you want to override that detected target.

## Key options

Some notable flags include:

- `--tap-client CLIENT` to choose which client to launch/listen to; `--tap-no-launch` to only start the proxy without launching a client; `--tap-output-dir DIR` for the trace output directory (default ./.traces); `--tap-port PORT` for the proxy port; and `--tap-max-traces N` to limit kept trace sessions (default 50).

## Security notes

A couple of important caveats worth highlighting:

- claude-tap redacts common authentication headers such as Authorization and x-api-key, but it does not globally scrub prompts, request bodies, or tool output.
- When running in no-launch mode, you MUST specify `--tap-host 127.0.0.1` to ensure both the proxy and frontend listen only on the loopback address. Without this flag, --tap-no-launch mode defaults to binding 0.0.0.0, exposing the port to the public network.

The project is fairly popular, with around 1.5k stars and 157 forks on GitHub.

You can find it on GitHub at `github.com/liaohch3/claude-tap` or on PyPI for more detailed documentation.

content.text:
查清楚了。claude-tap（liaohch3，~1.5k star，Python）跟 tracelab 撞的是同一个母题——**本地代理 + trace viewer 看 coding agent 的流量**——而且它在"捕获广度 + 分发成熟度"上明显走在前面。但两者的内核其实不一样。直接给你对比和结论。

## 重叠的部分（这些别再当卖点了）

claude-tap 已经做了、而且做得更广/更成熟的：

| 能力 | claude-tap | tracelab |
|---|---|---|
| 反向代理抓 cc/codex | ✅ | ✅ |
| 客户端覆盖 | **十几个**（Gemini/Cursor/Kimi/OpenCode/Pi/Hermes/Antigravity/CodeBuddy…）+ forward proxy 模式解 OAuth/多 provider/Bedrock SigV4 | 只有 cc + codex，纯 reverse |
| token 占比 / 分组 / 全文搜索 / 暗色 / i18n | ✅ | ✅ |
| 相邻请求 diff、看 context 变化 | ✅ | ✅（更语义化，见下） |
| 分发 | `pip/uv install`，**自包含单 HTML 产物**可直接发给别人 | 要 `go build` + 前端 build，无分发 |
| 本地 / auth 脱敏 | ✅ | ✅ |

**这一栏就是结论的前半句**：凡是"抓更多客户端 / viewer 更好看 / token 面板"，你都在跟一个广度更大、已经有 1.5k star 势能、还能一行 pip 装的对手正面打。这条路赢不了。

## tracelab 真正的差异点（claude-tap 结构上没有的）

这几条才是它不只是"分发更差的 claude-tap"的地方：

1. **Hook 作为一等数据源，并跟 HTTP 融合** —— 这是最大的本质差异。claude-tap 是**纯 HTTP 代理**，它只能看到线上的请求体。tracelab 收 `PreToolUse/PostToolUse/UserPromptSubmit` 这些 harness hook，并且把 **hook 时间线当成一轮的主干**，HTTP 退成 side sheet。你看到的是 agent 真实的 **tool 生命周期**，不是只有线缆流量。claude-tap 看不到 hook 层。

2. **结构化因果关联（hook ↔ HTTP，非启发式）** —— 基于 tool-call 生命周期严格定序去连 hook 和它对应的 completion。claude-tap 是"对比相邻请求、你自己找哪变了"。

3. **Replay（主动重放）** —— 把抓到的请求（可改 body）重新打回上游、归一化、原始 vs 重放对比。claude-tap **只抓不放**，是纯只读 inspector。这是 claude-tap 整条产品线都没有的方向。

4. **Replay library / eval 地基（/cases）** —— 同一个 case 跨 session/模型跑、对比矩阵。claude-tap 的相关延伸是喂给 Phistory 存 system prompt 快照（也是只读对比），没有主动 eval。

5. **provider-neutral 归一化层**（`internal/event` 边界 + typed blocks + 疑似标记 + 分 section token 占比）—— 一个真正的语义层，"数据怎么来"和"数据什么意思"解耦。claude-tap 是直接展示原始 trace + token 数字。

6. **持久化 DB（sqlite + cases 表）** vs claude-tap 的 jsonl + `--tap-max-traces 50` 上限。tracelab 是奔着"积累 + 查询 + 回归"设计的。

7. **语义化 context diff**（compaction 检测、变更分类）+ **click-to-launch（含 codex desktop）带正经安全模型**（opt-in / Origin 校验 / 配置备份恢复）。

## 结论

- **你不是在重复造 claude-tap，但你的"capture + viewer"那一半确实是在重复，而且是劣势侧。**
- tracelab 的护城河全部集中在 **hook 融合 + 因果关联 + 主动 replay + eval 矩阵** 这条线上——这恰好就是我上一轮说的 M4 方向。claude-tap 的架构（纯 HTTP 只读）决定了它**很难顺手长出 replay/eval**，这是你的结构性空间。
- 战术建议很明确：**不要在"支持更多客户端 / forward proxy / 更漂亮的 viewer"上跟它卷**——那是它的主场，你会一直落后。把叙事和精力压到 claude-tap 干不了的事上：**"不只是看 agent 发了什么，而是能把它重放、改参数、跨模型对比、做降智回归。"** 一句话定位差异：claude-tap 是**录像机**，tracelab 要做的是**录像机 + 剪辑台 + 回归测试台**。

要不要我顺手去把 claude-tap 的实际 viewer 截图/feature 列表（README、wiki）拉下来逐条核对一遍，确保上面"它没有 replay/hook"的判断没有漏掉它最近加的功能？它迭代挺快的，值得在你定方向前 double-check 一次。

Sources:
- [claude-tap (liaohch3)](https://github.com/liaohch3/claude-tap)
- [claude-tap wiki](https://github.com/liaohch3/claude-tap/wiki)
- [fork-claude-tap 架构说明](https://github.com/oneyoung19/fork-claude-tap)
- [claude-tap on PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/claude-tap/0.1.3/)

1	# Replay Library — 设计与现状
2	
3	> 模块定位：把"一条捕获到的模型请求"变成**可复用、可编辑、可重发**的素材，
4	> 围绕 `replay-note.md` 里的 6 个行为实验，沉淀出 codex / Claude Code 两种 wire
5	> 格式的代表性请求形状。最后更新：2026-06-23。
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. 它是什么
10	
11	Replay Library（前端称"素材库 / Replay library"）是一组保存下来的请求 case。
12	每个 case = **一条发给上游的请求体** + 路由元信息。选一个 case、选一个 live
13	session（提供凭证与上游路由）、（可选）改 body、点 Run，就会真实重发并把响应
14	按抓包同一套 normalize 流程解析出来。
15	
16	凭证**永不落盘**：case 只存请求体和 endpoint，auth 来自所选 session 的**内存**
17	headers。因此只能用本进程内启动过的 session 来跑 case。
18	
19	---
20	
21	## 2. 与 replay-note 实验的关系（重要约束）
22	
23	一个 case 只能复现**一条 HTTP 请求**，而 note 里的实验大多跨多次模型请求 +
24	harness 编排（权限、diff 生成、compaction、子 agent 调度）。这些**编排逻辑无法
25	靠重放单个请求复现**。我们的做法是：把每个实验**定格成 agent loop 里最有代表性
26	的那一条请求**，做成 case。
27	
28	| 实验 | 做成的 case | 能复现 | 不能复现 |
29	|---|---|---|---|
30	| 1 纯文本 | 带完整工具目录但要求"只回 hello" | 首请求形状 | 事件路径 |
31	| 2 读文件 | `tool_use`+`tool_result` / `function_call`+`output` 回灌 | tool result 如何进下一次请求 | PreToolUse 时序 |
32	| 3 改文件 | codex 独有 `custom_tool_call`(apply_patch) | apply_patch 的 freeform 形状 | 权限 / diff 来源（harness 行为） |
33	| 4 执行失败 | `tool_result is_error` / 非零退出 output | 失败如何编码、模型如何重试 | 输出截断（harness 行为） |
34	| 5 compaction | —（无真实捕获，已跳过） | — | — |
35	| 6 子 agent | —（无真实捕获，已跳过） | — | — |
36	
37	实验 5/6 在现有 319 条捕获里**找不到任何真实 compaction / subagent 请求**
38	（强标记 grep 全空），按"有就做没有跳过"的原则未做，等实跑这些场景捕获后再补。
39	
40	---
41	
42	## 3. 架构
43	
44	依赖方向：`routes/ui → viewmodel → (query + store) → api`（前端 MVVM 规范）。
45	
46	### 数据模型（`internal/store/cases.go`）
47	
48	```
49	ReplayCase { ID, Name, Tags, Provider, Method, Target, Endpoint, Body, Source, CreatedAt }
50	```
51	
52	- `Source`：`seed`（内置）｜ `captured`（从抓到的事件保存）｜ `snapshot`（编辑后另存的派生）｜ `manual`（手填）。
53	- `Endpoint`：session 相对路径（如 `/responses`、`/v1/messages`）。case 只拥有
54	  请求形状 + endpoint，**上游由 session 提供**（`Target` 仅作展示元信息）。
55	- 表：`replay_cases`（`internal/store/schema.sql`）。
56	
57	### 后端流程
58	
59	- **列表 / 增删**：`internal/server/cases_api.go`
60	  - `handleAddCase`：从捕获事件保存（读 `request_body` raw 文件），或手填（`handleCreateManualCase`，会猜 provider/endpoint）。
61	  - `handleSnapshotCase`：编辑后**另存为新 case**，原 case 不动。
62	  - `handleOverwriteCase`：编辑后**就地覆盖**同一 case（不可撤销，UI 二次确认）。
63	  - `handleDeleteCase`：seed 也可删（库归用户所有）。
64	- **运行**：`handleRunCase` → `executeCaseThroughSession`（`internal/server/replay.go`）：
65	  走真实客户端同款 session 代理 `/s/<token>/<endpoint>`，session 注入 auth、
66	  决定上游，响应经 `reg.Normalize` 解析。非 2xx 时把原始 body 一并回传
67	  （`replayResp.ResponseBody`），避免错误显示成空白。
68	- **curl 导出**：`handleCaseCurl`（`internal/server/case_curl.go`）两种模式：
69	  `proxy`（打 tracelab 代理，不含密钥）/ `direct`（直连含 auth，默认打码）。
70	
71	### 种子机制（once-per-database）
72	
73	`seedCases()` 用 `schema_meta.cases_seeded` 标记**每个库只 seed 一次**。代价：
74	用户删掉的内置 case 重启后不会复活，但新版本新增的 seed 也不会自动出现在旧库里
75	（库 = 用户所有）。**给旧库刷新 seed** 的办法：
76	
77	```sql
78	DELETE FROM replay_cases WHERE id LIKE 'seed:%';
79	DELETE FROM schema_meta WHERE key='cases_seeded';
80	-- 然后重启 tracelab 触发重新 seed
81	```
82	
83	> ⚠️ 这会丢掉用户对内置 case 的删除/覆盖；只删 `seed:%` 行，不影响自建 case。
84	
85	### 前端
86	
87	- `ui/CasesPanel.tsx`：**卡片画廊**——顶部 provider 过滤（`ProviderFilter`，状态在
88	  `store/ui.ts` 的 `casesProvider`），下方按 内置 / 后加 分组的卡片网格
89	  （`CaseCard`：provider icon + 标题 + 实验说明 + 核心字段 method/endpoint/model/
90	  tools/stream + source 徽标 + 删除）。点卡片在 Dialog 里打开 CaseRunner（选
91	  session、编辑 body、Run、覆盖、快照、curl、结果 Sheet）。
92	- `viewmodel/cases.ts`：`caseSections` 分组、`filterCasesByProvider` / `caseProviders`
93	  过滤、`caseProviderClient`（provider→品牌 icon）、`caseCardMeta`（从 body 抽
94	  model/tools/stream）、`caseDescription`（内置实验说明 i18n）、`caseEndpoint` /
95	  `caseRunURL` 派生、`caseDisplayName(c, t)` 把内置标题映射到 i18n（见 §5）。
96	- `query/cases.ts`：TanStack Query hooks（`useCases` / `useRunCase` / `useSnapshotCase`
97	  / `useOverwriteCase` / `useDeleteCase` / `useCreateCase` / `useCaseCurl`）。
98	
99	---
100	
101	## 4. 内置 seed 集（9 个）
102	
103	文件在 `internal/store/seeds/*.json`，用 `//go:embed` 进二进制。
104	
105	| seed id | provider | endpoint | 实验 | 文件 |
106	|---|---|---|---|---|
107	| `seed:codex-pure-text` | openai-responses | /responses | 1 | codex-pure-text.json |
108	| `seed:codex-tool-read` | openai-responses | /responses | 2 | codex-tool-read.json |
109	| `seed:codex-apply-patch` | openai-responses | /responses | 3 | codex-apply-patch.json |
110	| `seed:codex-tool-failure` | openai-responses | /responses | 4 | codex-tool-failure.json |
111	| `seed:cc-pure-text` | anthropic | /v1/messages | 1 | cc-pure-text.json |
112	| `seed:cc-tool-read` | anthropic | /v1/messages | 2 | cc-tool-read.json |
113	| `seed:cc-tool-failure` | anthropic | /v1/messages | 4 | cc-tool-failure.json |
114	| `seed:cc-title` | anthropic | /v1/messages | 附赠：会话标题生成 | cc-title.json |
115	| `seed:cc-full-claude` | anthropic | /v1/messages | 完整请求形状（cache_control + adaptive thinking） | cc-full-messages.json |
116	
117	合并/取舍：
118	- 实验 2 与 3 在"单请求形状"上同构（都是工具调用 → 结果回灌），cc 侧合并为一个
119	  read 回灌；codex 侧因 `apply_patch` 是 **custom freeform grammar 工具**（形状
120	  确实不同）单列。
121	- codex 无子 agent，实验 6 对 codex 不适用。
122	
123	### seed 来源与脱敏（混合策略）
124	
125	发现：一条 362B 的真实 codex 请求（无 `tools`、`instructions` 仅一句）也被
126	chatgpt backend 200 接受 → 说明 codex 的 `tools` 和长 `instructions` **可选**。
127	据此采用混合构造，兼顾"被 API 接受"与"体积 / 隐私"：
128	
129	- **纯文本**：用已验证的最小真实形状（model/instructions/`input[input_text]`/
130	  `store:false`/`stream:true`/`include:[reasoning.encrypted_content]`）。
131	- **工具 / 失败 / apply_patch**：短 instructions/system + **从真实捕获提取的单个
132	  工具 schema**（codex `exec_command`/`apply_patch`；cc `Read`/`Bash`/`Edit`，
133	  均为厂商公开定义、非用户 PII）+ **手写的无害剧本**（读 README、跑 go test 等）。
134	- **cc-title**：直接取真实标题生成请求，替换会话内容 + 删 `metadata`。
135	
136	每个 seed 1.7–7KB。**彻底脱敏**：丢弃 codex 的 `client_metadata`/`prompt_cache_key`
137	（含 installation_id、session_id、workspace 路径、git hash）和 cc 的
138	`metadata.user_id`（device_id/account_uuid）；路径统一 `/workspace`；真实邮箱 →
139	`example@example.com`。逐 token 复查无 `/Users/`、真实邮箱、姓名、`tracelab` 残留。
140	（`noreply@anthropic.com` 是 Bash 工具 schema 自带的公共地址，保留。）
141	
142	> ⚠️ codex `chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses` 是订阅路径；旧 seed 曾指向
143	> `api.openai.com/v1/responses`，对订阅账号必 401，且 body 过简会 400——已废弃。
144	
145	---
146	
147	## 5. i18n
148	
149	内置标题不硬编码语言：DB 里 `Name` 存英文 fallback，前端 `caseDisplayName`
150	按 `cases.seed.<id>`（id 去掉 `seed:` 前缀、`-`→`_`）取 i18n，随语言切换。
151	key 在 `frontend/src/i18n/{en,zh}.json` 的 `cases.seed.*`。用户自建 case 用原名。
152	
153	---
154	
155	## 6. UI 行为约定
156	
157	- **覆盖 / 另存快照**按钮**始终可见**，未编辑 body 时禁用（`cases.edit_to_enable`
158	  悬停提示）——避免"编辑前不渲染导致找不到"。
159	- Run 前二次确认（真实计费请求）。
160	- direct 模式 curl 默认打码，需显式 reveal。
161	
162	---
163	
164	## 7. 已知缺口 / 后续
165	
166	- [ ] 实验 5（compaction）、6（子 agent）：等实跑捕获到真实请求后，从捕获保存为
167	      case（或补 seed）。手搓风险高（易被拒），不预先做。
168	- [ ] seed 是否被上游接受，需在 live session 下逐个 Run 验证（本进程无凭证无法自验）；
169	      形状均来自被 200 接受过的真实字段，置信度高，万一 400 错误体会显示在结果里。
170	- [ ] 旧库刷新 seed 需手动重置标志（见 §3），尚无版本化迁移。
171	
172	---
173	
174	## 8. 关键文件索引
175	
176	```
177	internal/store/cases.go            数据模型、seedCases、EndpointForTarget
178	internal/store/seeds/*.json        9 个内置请求体
179	internal/store/schema.sql          replay_cases 表
180	internal/server/cases_api.go       list/add/snapshot/overwrite/delete/run handler
181	internal/server/replay.go          executeReplay / executeCaseThroughSession / buildReplayEvent
182	internal/server/case_curl.go       curl 导出（proxy / direct）
183	frontend/src/ui/CasesPanel.tsx     列表 + CaseRunner + CurlSheet
184	frontend/src/viewmodel/cases.ts    分组 / endpoint / caseDisplayName
185	frontend/src/query/cases.ts        TanStack Query hooks
186	frontend/src/api/cases.ts          DTO + fetch 封装
187	frontend/src/i18n/{en,zh}.json     cases.* 文案（含 seed.*）
188	```
189

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		_ "embed"
5		"net/url"
6		"strings"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	// Built-in case bodies, shipped as files so the realistic (and large) wire shapes
11	// stay readable and diffable. Each is a genuine request shape — derived from real
12	// captured codex/claude traffic, then stripped of credentials, identifiers, and any
13	// user content — so it is accepted by upstream instead of 400'd like a toy payload.
14	// They map to the replay-note experiments: pure text, a tool read round-trip, a file
15	// edit, and a failed tool result, for both wire formats (plus a CC session-title
16	// generation request and the original full-shape CC message).
17	//
18	//go:embed seeds/cc-full-messages.json
19	var ccFullMessagesBody string
20	
21	//go:embed seeds/codex-pure-text.json
22	var codexPureTextBody string
23	
24	//go:embed seeds/codex-tool-read.json
25	var codexToolReadBody string
26	
27	//go:embed seeds/codex-apply-patch.json
28	var codexApplyPatchBody string
29	
30	//go:embed seeds/codex-tool-failure.json
31	var codexToolFailureBody string
32	
33	//go:embed seeds/cc-pure-text.json
34	var ccPureTextBody string
35	
36	//go:embed seeds/cc-tool-read.json
37	var ccToolReadBody string
38	
39	//go:embed seeds/cc-tool-failure.json
40	var ccToolFailureBody string
41	
42	//go:embed seeds/cc-title.json
43	var ccTitleBody string
44	
45	// ReplayCase is one saved, re-sendable request in the replay library.
46	type ReplayCase struct {
47		ID        string `json:"id"`
48		Name      string `json:"name"`
49		Tags      string `json:"tags"`
50		Provider  string `json:"provider"`
51		Method    string `json:"method"`
52		Target    string `json:"target"`
53		Endpoint  string `json:"endpoint"`
54		Body      string `json:"body"`
55		Source    string `json:"source"` // seed | captured | snapshot
56		CreatedAt string `json:"created_at"`
57	}
58	
59	// ListCases returns all replay-library cases, newest first.
60	func (s *Store) ListCases() ([]ReplayCase, error) {
61		rows, err := s.db.Query(
62			`SELECT id, name, tags, provider, method, target, COALESCE(endpoint,''), body, source, created_at
63			 FROM replay_cases ORDER BY created_at DESC, name`)
64		if err != nil {
65			return nil, err
66		}
67		defer rows.Close()
68		out := []ReplayCase{}
69		for rows.Next() {
70			var c ReplayCase
71			if err := rows.Scan(&c.ID, &c.Name, &c.Tags, &c.Provider, &c.Method, &c.Target, &c.Endpoint, &c.Body, &c.Source, &c.CreatedAt); err != nil {
72				return nil, err
73			}
74			out = append(out, c)
75		}
76		return out, rows.Err()
77	}
78	
79	// GetCase returns one case or sql.ErrNoRows.
80	func (s *Store) GetCase(id string) (*ReplayCase, error) {
81		var c ReplayCase
82		err := s.db.QueryRow(
83			`SELECT id, name, tags, provider, method, target, COALESCE(endpoint,''), body, source, created_at
84			 FROM replay_cases WHERE id = ?`, id).Scan(
85			&c.ID, &c.Name, &c.Tags, &c.Provider, &c.Method, &c.Target, &c.Endpoint, &c.Body, &c.Source, &c.CreatedAt)
86		if err != nil {
87			return nil, err
88		}
89		return &c, nil
90	}
91	
92	// AddCase inserts (or replaces) a case.
93	func (s *Store) AddCase(c ReplayCase) error {
94		if c.CreatedAt == "" {
95			c.CreatedAt = time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)
96		}
97		if c.Endpoint == "" {
98			c.Endpoint = EndpointForTarget(c.Provider, c.Target, "")
99		}
100		_, err := s.db.Exec(
101			`INSERT OR REPLACE INTO replay_cases(id, name, tags, provider, method, target, endpoint, body, source, created_at)
102			 VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`,
103			c.ID, c.Name, c.Tags, c.Provider, c.Method, c.Target, c.Endpoint, c.Body, c.Source, c.CreatedAt)
104		return err
105	}
106	
107	// DeleteCase removes a case by id. Seed (built-in) cases are protected at the
108	// handler layer, not here, so this stays a plain delete usable by tests.
109	func (s *Store) DeleteCase(id string) error {
110		_, err := s.db.Exec(`DELETE FROM replay_cases WHERE id = ?`, id)
111		return err
112	}
113	
114	// seedCases installs predefined cases ONCE per database. A schema_meta flag marks
115	// that seeding has happened, so cases the user deletes — built-in ones included —
116	// stay deleted across restarts instead of resurrecting. Trade-off: seeds added by
117	// a later tracelab version won't auto-appear in an existing library (the library
118	// becomes fully user-owned after first run). The simplest experiment: ask the
119	// model "who are you" — one per wire format.
120	func (s *Store) seedCases() error {
121		var seeded string
122		if err := s.db.QueryRow(
123			`SELECT value FROM schema_meta WHERE key = 'cases_seeded'`).Scan(&seeded); err == nil && seeded == "1" {
124			return nil
125		}
126		const (
127			codexTarget = "https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses"
128			ccTarget    = "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages"
129		)
130		codexCase := func(id, name, tags, body string) ReplayCase {
131			return ReplayCase{ID: id, Name: name, Tags: tags, Provider: "openai-responses",
132				Method: "POST", Target: codexTarget, Endpoint: "/responses", Body: body, Source: "seed"}
133		}
134		ccCase := func(id, name, tags, body string) ReplayCase {
135			return ReplayCase{ID: id, Name: name, Tags: tags, Provider: "anthropic",
136				Method: "POST", Target: ccTarget, Endpoint: "/v1/messages", Body: body, Source: "seed"}
137		}
138		// Names here are English fallbacks; the UI shows a localized title keyed by the
139		// seed id (cases.seed.<id>) so built-in titles aren't hardcoded to one language.
140		seeds := []ReplayCase{
141			// codex (openai-responses) — note experiments 1–4
142			codexCase("seed:codex-pure-text", "Plain text · no tools (codex)", "text,smoke,codex", codexPureTextBody),
143			codexCase("seed:codex-tool-read", "Tool round-trip · read file (codex)", "tool,codex", codexToolReadBody),
144			codexCase("seed:codex-apply-patch", "Edit file · apply_patch (codex)", "tool,edit,codex", codexApplyPatchBody),
145			codexCase("seed:codex-tool-failure", "Tool failure · error feedback (codex)", "tool,error,codex", codexToolFailureBody),
146			// claude code (anthropic) — note experiments 1, 2, 4 + extras
147			ccCase("seed:cc-pure-text", "Plain text · no tools (cc)", "text,smoke,cc", ccPureTextBody),
148			ccCase("seed:cc-tool-read", "Tool round-trip · read file (cc)", "tool,cc", ccToolReadBody),
149			ccCase("seed:cc-tool-failure", "Tool failure · error feedback (cc)", "tool,error,cc", ccToolFailureBody),
150			ccCase("seed:cc-title", "Session title generation (cc)", "text,cc", ccTitleBody),
151			ccCase("seed:cc-full-claude", "Full request shape (cc)", "text,cc,smoke", ccFullMessagesBody),
152		}
153		now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)
154		for _, c := range seeds {
155			if _, err := s.db.Exec(
156				`INSERT OR IGNORE INTO replay_cases(id, name, tags, provider, method, target, endpoint, body, source, created_at)
157				 VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`,
158				c.ID, c.Name, c.Tags, c.Provider, c.Method, c.Target, c.Endpoint, c.Body, c.Source, now); err != nil {
159				return err
160			}
161		}
162		_, err := s.db.Exec(
163			`INSERT INTO schema_meta(key, value) VALUES('cases_seeded','1')
164			 ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value = '1'`)
165		return err
166	}
167	
168	// EndpointForTarget turns an absolute captured target into the path that should
169	// be replayed through a launch session. The session owns the upstream; the case
170	// owns only the request shape and endpoint.
171	func EndpointForTarget(provider, target, upstream string) string {
172		cleanUpstream := strings.TrimRight(upstream, "/")
173		if cleanUpstream != "" && strings.HasPrefix(target, cleanUpstream+"/") {
174			return ensureSlash(strings.TrimPrefix(target, cleanUpstream))
175		}
176	
177		u, err := url.Parse(target)
178		if err != nil {
179			return ensureSlash(target)
180		}
181		path := u.EscapedPath()
182		if u.RawQuery != "" {
183			path += "?" + u.RawQuery
184		}
185	
186		p := strings.ToLower(provider)
187		if strings.Contains(p, "openai") && strings.HasSuffix(u.EscapedPath(), "/responses") {
188			return "/responses"
189		}
190		if strings.Contains(p, "anthropic") && strings.Contains(u.EscapedPath(), "/v1/messages") {
191			return "/v1/messages"
192		}
193		return ensureSlash(path)
194	}
195	
196	func ensureSlash(s string) string {
197		if s == "" || s[0] == '/' {
198			return s
199		}
200		return "/" + s
201	}

1	-- tracelab module 2 schema (version 1).
2	-- Design: a shared "spine" (events) for the unified session timeline, with
3	-- type-specific detail tables (http_exchanges / hook_events) so the two very
4	-- different data sources evolve independently without NULL-soup. Raw bytes live
5	-- on the filesystem (kept as a user-facing feature); the DB only points to them.
6	
7	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_meta (
8	  key   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
9	  value TEXT
10	);
11	
12	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
13	  id         TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
14	  client     TEXT,
15	  upstream   TEXT,
16	  label      TEXT,
17	  started_at TEXT,
18	  ended_at   TEXT,
19	  meta_json  TEXT
20	);
21	
22	-- Spine: the minimal set every event shares (timeline + correlation + source).
23	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (
24	  id             TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
25	  session_id     TEXT REFERENCES sessions(id),
26	  kind           TEXT,                 -- http_exchange | hook_event
27	  source_adapter TEXT,
28	  source_mode    TEXT,
29	  client         TEXT,
30	  turn_id        TEXT,
31	  parent_id      TEXT,
32	  request_id     TEXT,
33	  started_at     TEXT,
34	  completed_at   TEXT,
35	  duration_ms    INTEGER,
36	  created_at      TEXT
37	);
38	
39	-- HTTP-specific detail (1:1 with events where kind=http_exchange).
40	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS http_exchanges (
41	  event_id        TEXT PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES events(id),
42	  method          TEXT,
43	  url             TEXT,
44	  target          TEXT,
45	  response_status INTEGER,
46	  is_completion   INTEGER,             -- 1=completion call  0=control/probe
47	  provider        TEXT,
48	  model           TEXT,
49	  wire_api        TEXT,
50	  normalize_error TEXT,
51	  input_tokens    INTEGER,
52	  output_tokens   INTEGER,
53	  total_tokens    INTEGER,
54	  cached_tokens   INTEGER,
55	  reasoning_tokens INTEGER,
56	  normalized_json TEXT                  -- block.Raw stripped (full original is on disk)
57	);
58	
59	-- Hook-specific detail (1:1 with events where kind=hook_event).
60	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS hook_events (
61	  event_id   TEXT PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES events(id),
62	  runtime    TEXT,
63	  event_name TEXT,
64	  tool_call_id TEXT,
65	  tool_name  TEXT                  -- tool a Pre/PostToolUse hook refers to
66	);
67	
68	-- Pointers to raw bytes on disk (filesystem storage, kept as a feature).
69	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS raw_files (
70	  id               TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
71	  event_id         TEXT REFERENCES events(id),
72	  role             TEXT,               -- request_body | response_body | hook_payload
73	  path             TEXT,               -- relative to data dir; user can open directly
74	  media_type       TEXT,
75	  content_encoding TEXT,
76	  body_encoding    TEXT,
77	  size_bytes       INTEGER,
78	  redaction_applied INTEGER
79	);
80	
81	-- Tags: structural facts, suspected heuristics, and (later) manual labels.
82	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tags (
83	  id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
84	  event_id   TEXT REFERENCES events(id),
85	  tag        TEXT,
86	  confidence REAL,
87	  suspected  INTEGER,                  -- 1 = shown as 疑似
88	  source     TEXT,                     -- structural | heuristic | manual
89	  evidence   TEXT,
90	  created_at TEXT
91	);
92	
93	-- Replay library: saved, re-sendable requests (predefined seeds + ones the user
94	-- adds from captured traffic). A case is provider-neutral request material; it is
95	-- run against a chosen session that supplies the credentials (next.md module 4).
96	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS replay_cases (
97	  id         TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
98	  name       TEXT,
99	  tags       TEXT,                -- comma-separated scenario labels
100	  provider   TEXT,                -- best-effort label (anthropic / openai-responses…)
101	  method     TEXT,
102	  target     TEXT,                -- absolute upstream URL
103	  endpoint   TEXT,                -- session-relative path, e.g. /responses or /v1/messages
104	  body       TEXT,                -- request body
105	  source     TEXT,                -- seed | captured
106	  created_at TEXT
107	);
108	
109	-- Per-client hook event registry: which lifecycle/tool events tracelab wires
110	-- when it launches each coding agent. Seeded with the built-in defaults (one row
111	-- per event, source='seed', kept current against each runtime's docs); the
112	-- launcher reads the ENABLED rows here, so users can add a newly-shipped event
113	-- (source='user') or disable a noisy one (enabled=0) from the UI without waiting
114	-- for a tracelab release. Distinct from hook_events, which stores CAPTURED hooks.
115	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS client_hook_events (
116	  client  TEXT NOT NULL,        -- claude_code | codex
117	  event   TEXT NOT NULL,        -- PreToolUse, SessionStart, …
118	  enabled INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
119	  source  TEXT NOT NULL,        -- seed | user
120	  PRIMARY KEY(client, event)
121	);
122	
123	-- Per-section token stats (http only) for queries like "tools > 50% of tokens".
124	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sections (
125	  event_id      TEXT REFERENCES events(id),
126	  name          TEXT,
127	  bytes         INTEGER,
128	  approx_tokens INTEGER
129	);
130	
131	-- Full-text search over the meaningful prompt sections (next.md 3.2).
132	CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events_fts USING fts5(
133	  event_id UNINDEXED,
134	  system_text,
135	  messages_text,
136	  final_text,
137	  tool_args_text,
138	  tokenize='unicode61'
139	);
140	
141	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_session ON events(session_id, started_at);
142	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_http_provider  ON http_exchanges(provider, model);
143	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tags_tag        ON tags(tag);
144	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tags_event      ON tags(event_id);

1	// Package normalize turns provider-agnostic SourceEvents into a provider-neutral
2	// semantic model (NormalizedEnvelope). Each provider gets its own sub-package
3	// implementing Normalizer; they share only the atomic helpers in
4	// normalize/shared. Adding a provider must not touch existing ones.
5	package normalize
6	
7	import "encoding/json"
8	
9	// EnvelopeSchemaVersion identifies the normalized model revision.
10	const EnvelopeSchemaVersion = "tracelab.normalized.v1"
11	
12	// BlockType classifies a content block. The type is always derived from the
13	// provider's own structural type field — never guessed from keywords.
14	type BlockType string
15	
16	const (
17		BlockText       BlockType = "text"
18		BlockReasoning  BlockType = "reasoning"
19		BlockToolCall   BlockType = "tool_call"
20		BlockToolResult BlockType = "tool_result"
21		BlockImage      BlockType = "image"
22		BlockUnknown    BlockType = "unknown" // structurally present but unmapped
23	)
24	
25	// ContentBlock is one typed unit of message content. Raw always holds the
26	// original provider block so nothing is lost and power users can drill in.
27	type ContentBlock struct {
28		Type       BlockType       `json:"type"`
29		Text       string          `json:"text,omitempty"`
30		ToolCall   *ToolCall       `json:"tool_call,omitempty"`
31		ToolResult *ToolResult     `json:"tool_result,omitempty"`
32		Raw        json.RawMessage `json:"raw,omitempty"`
33	}
34	
35	// Message is a role-tagged sequence of typed content blocks.
36	type Message struct {
37		Role    string         `json:"role"`
38		Content []ContentBlock `json:"content,omitempty"`
39	}
40	
41	// Tool is a provider-neutral tool/function definition.
42	type Tool struct {
43		Name        string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
44		Description string          `json:"description,omitempty"`
45		InputSchema json.RawMessage `json:"input_schema,omitempty"`
46	}
47	
48	// ToolCall is a model request to invoke a tool.
49	type ToolCall struct {
50		ID        string          `json:"id,omitempty"`
51		Name      string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
52		Arguments json.RawMessage `json:"arguments,omitempty"`
53	}
54	
55	// ToolResult is the result fed back for a tool call.
56	type ToolResult struct {
57		ToolCallID string         `json:"tool_call_id,omitempty"`
58		Content    []ContentBlock `json:"content,omitempty"`
59		IsError    bool           `json:"is_error,omitempty"`
60	}
61	
62	// Usage is token accounting. Provider-specific extras (cache tokens, tiers) go
63	// in Extra so the neutral fields stay comparable across providers.
64	type Usage struct {
65		InputTokens  int64          `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
66		OutputTokens int64          `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
67		TotalTokens  int64          `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"`
68		Extra        map[string]any `json:"extra,omitempty"`
69	}
70	
71	// SectionStat is the size of one request section (system / tools / messages).
72	// ApproxTokens is explicitly approximate (see shared.ApproxTokens).
73	type SectionStat struct {
74		Name         string `json:"name"`
75		Bytes        int64  `json:"bytes"`
76		ApproxTokens int64  `json:"approx_tokens"`
77	}
78	
79	// TagSignal is a structural hint about what an exchange contains. Signals that
80	// cannot be determined structurally MUST set Suspected=true so the UI can show
81	// them as "疑似" rather than fact (see next.md 3.3).
82	type TagSignal struct {
83		Tag        string  `json:"tag"`
84		Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"`
85		Suspected  bool    `json:"suspected,omitempty"`
86		Evidence   string  `json:"evidence,omitempty"`
87	}
88	
89	// ProviderRef names the detected provider and model. Name is the normalizer's
90	// own id (e.g. "anthropic", "openai-responses"), not a guess.
91	type ProviderRef struct {
92		Name     string `json:"name"`
93		Model    string `json:"model,omitempty"`
94		Endpoint string `json:"endpoint,omitempty"`
95		WireAPI  string `json:"wire_api,omitempty"`
96	}
97	
98	// RequestEnvelope is the provider-neutral request model.
99	type RequestEnvelope struct {
100		System     []ContentBlock `json:"system,omitempty"`
101		Messages   []Message      `json:"messages,omitempty"`
102		Tools      []Tool         `json:"tools,omitempty"`
103		Parameters map[string]any `json:"parameters,omitempty"`
104		Sections   []SectionStat  `json:"sections,omitempty"`
105	}
106	
107	// ResponseEnvelope is the provider-neutral response model.
108	type ResponseEnvelope struct {
109		Output     []Message  `json:"output,omitempty"`
110		FinalText  string     `json:"final_text,omitempty"`
111		ToolCalls  []ToolCall `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`
112		Usage      *Usage     `json:"usage,omitempty"`
113		StopReason string     `json:"stop_reason,omitempty"`
114	}
115	
116	// NormalizedEnvelope is the full semantic view downstream modules consume.
117	type NormalizedEnvelope struct {
118		SchemaVersion string            `json:"schema_version"`
119		EventID       string            `json:"event_id"`
120		Provider      ProviderRef       `json:"provider"`
121		Request       *RequestEnvelope  `json:"request,omitempty"`
122		Response      *ResponseEnvelope `json:"response,omitempty"`
123		Signals       []TagSignal       `json:"signals,omitempty"`
124		Metadata      map[string]any    `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
125	}
126

26				Query:    q.Get("q"),
27				Tag:      q.Get("tag"),
28				Provider: q.Get("provider"),

30			})
31			if err != nil {

33				return
34			}
35			writeJSON(w, results)
36		})

56		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/launch", s.handleLaunch)

58		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /api/terminals", s.handleTerminals)

60		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/codex-desktop/install", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { s.handleCodexDesktopInstall(st, w, r) })
61		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/codex-desktop/restore", s.handleCodexDesktopRestore)
62		// In-memory sessions = the ones still replayable in this process (have creds).

76		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/cases", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { s.handleAddCase(st, w, r) })

78		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/cases/{id}/snapshot", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { s.handleSnapshotCase(st, w, r) })
79		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/cases/{id}/overwrite", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { s.handleOverwriteCase(st, w, r) })
80		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/cases/{id}/curl", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { s.handleCaseCurl(st, w, r) })

82		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /api/hook-events", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { s.handleListHookEvents(st, w, r) })

85		s.mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /api/hook-events", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { s.handleDeleteHookEvent(st, w, r) })
86		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /api/sessions/{id}/events", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {

103				return

106		})

108			path, err := st.RawFilePath(r.PathValue("id"), r.PathValue("role"))
109			if err == store.ErrNoRows {

122	// exists. SPA routes fall back to index.html.
123	func (s *Server) EnableViewer(distDir string) bool {
124		if st, err := os.Stat(distDir); err != nil || !st.IsDir() {
125			return false
126		}
127		fs := http.FileServer(http.Dir(distDir))
128		index := filepath.Join(distDir, "index.html")
129		s.mux.HandleFunc("/viewer/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
130			r.URL.Path = strings.TrimPrefix(r.URL.Path, "/viewer")
131			// SPA fallback: serve real files (assets) as-is, but route paths like
132			// /sessions/:id — which have no file on disk — get index.html so client
133			// routing can take over on deep links and reloads.
134			rel := strings.TrimPrefix(filepath.Clean(r.URL.Path), "/")
135			if rel == "" || rel == "." {
136				http.ServeFile(w, r, index)
137				return
138			}
139			if st, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(distDir, rel)); err != nil || st.IsDir() {
140				http.ServeFile(w, r, index)
141				return
142			}
143			fs.ServeHTTP(w, r)
144		})
145		s.mux.HandleFunc("/viewer", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
146			http.Redirect(w, r, "/viewer/", http.StatusFound)
147		})
148		return true
149	}

questions.question: M4 v1 的断言引擎，先做到哪一层？这直接决定首版工作量和是否引入额外计费。

options.label: 只做确定性断言（推荐）
options.description: 对 normalized envelope 做结构化判定：final_text contains/regex、tool_call 名称命中、stop_reason==、output/total tokens 阈值、latency 阈值、JSONPath。零额外计费、可复现。LLM-judge 留到 v2。

options.label: 确定性 + LLM-judge
options.description: 额外加一种「用模型给答案质量打分」的断言（语义降智更敏感），但每个 cell 多一次计费调用、引入评委 prompt/模型选择，首版更重。

options.label: 只做 LLM-judge
options.description: 只用模型评分，跳过确定性断言。最贴近「答案变笨没变笨」，但不可复现、全程计费、且偏离项目「结构化非启发式」的基调。

questions.question: 降智/回归对比，首版主轴是哪个方向？决定是否首版就要引入历史/调度。

options.label: 同批次跨变体（推荐）
options.description: 一次 batch 内：同一 case 跑 模型A vs 模型B vs 不同参数，得到对比矩阵。无需历史/调度即可出价值。回归（跨时间）作为 v2 在持久化结果上叠加。

options.label: 跨时间回归
options.description: 同一配置定期重跑，对比已保存的 baseline（token/断言/文本漂移）。更贴近「上周还行这周变笨」，但首版就要引入结果历史 + baseline 选择 + 可能的调度。

options.label: 两者都要（首版）
options.description: 首版同时做跨变体矩阵和跨时间回归。最完整但工作量最大，落地周期拉长。

questions.question: 批量执行（真实计费、可能几十次调用）的运行方式？

options.label: 异步 job + 进度轮询（推荐）
options.description: 提交 batch 返回 run_id，后台串行/限并发执行，前端轮询进度与逐 cell 结果。大矩阵体验好，符合「真实计费需二次确认 + 可观察」。

options.label: 同步阻塞一次返回
options.description: 一个请求里把整个矩阵跑完再返回。实现最简单，但大矩阵会长时间挂起、无进度、易超时。

作为工作台，它当前这套已经很完整了：抓 → hook 融合时间线 → 因果关联 → context diff → 单条 replay（可改 body 重打、对比)。这套闭环对"搞懂 coding agent 怎么跟模型交互"是够用且自洽的，replay 的"改一下重打看差异"本来就是研究台最顺手的动作,而不是评测台的批量跑分。

 - Fast mode for Claude Code uses Claude Opus with faster output (it does not downgrade to a smaller model). It can be toggled with /fast and is available on Opus 4.8/4.7/4.6.

# Scratchpad Directory

IMPORTANT: Always use this scratchpad directory for temporary files instead of `/tmp` or other system temp directories:
`/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-you-personal-tracelab/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/scratchpad`

Use this directory for ALL temporary file needs:
- Storing intermediate results or data during multi-step tasks

Only use `/tmp` if the user explicitly requests it.

The scratchpad directory is session-specific, isolated from the user's project, and can generally be used without permission prompts.
